name: GitHub Connector
description: Browse your GitHub repositories, view code and commit history, and manage pull requests and issues directly from chat.

tools.name: start_github_install
tools.description: Get the link to connect GitHub — opens GitHub's own installation page where you pick which repositories to grant access to. Use this when the user wants to connect/link their GitHub account.

install_url.description: Open this URL to install/authorize the GitHub App on your chosen repositories
install_url.title: Install Url

tools.name: disconnect_github
tools.description: Disconnect your GitHub account — removes the stored installation record. GitHub's own App installation is not touched (uninstall it from github.com/settings/installations if you also want that gone); this just makes Imperal forget about it. Requires an explicit confirm=true on a second call — the first call only previews.

confirm.description: Set true on a second call to actually disconnect. First call (default) only previews.
confirm.title: Confirm

tools.name: list_repositories
tools.description: List the GitHub repositories this account's App installation has access to.

tools.name: get_file_contents
tools.description: Read a file's content, or list a directory's entries, from a GitHub repository at a given ref (branch/tag/commit). Pass path='' for the repo root.

path.description: File or directory path within the repo, e.g. 'src/app.py'. Empty string = repo root.
path.title: Path

ref.description: Branch, tag, or commit SHA. Empty = the repo's default branch.
ref.title: Ref

tools.name: list_recent_commits
tools.description: List recent commits on a GitHub repository, optionally filtered to those touching a specific path.

path.description: Optional: only commits touching this file/path
path.title: Path

tools.name: list_contributors
tools.description: List contributors to a GitHub repository, ranked by number of commits.

repo.description: Repository full name, e.g. 'owner/repo' — from list_repositories
repo.title: Repo

tools.name: list_pull_requests
tools.description: List pull requests on a connected GitHub repository (open by default).

tools.name: create_pull_request
tools.description: Open a new pull request in a connected GitHub repository (head branch into base branch).

tools.name: merge_pull_request
tools.description: Merge an open pull request in a connected GitHub repository. This is irreversible on GitHub's side, so it requires an explicit confirm=true on a second call — the first call only previews the merge.

confirm.description: Set true on a second call to actually merge. First call (default) only previews.
confirm.title: Confirm

tools.name: list_issues
tools.description: List issues on a connected GitHub repository (open by default). Pull requests are excluded — use list_pull_requests for those.

tools.name: create_issue
tools.description: Open a new issue in a connected GitHub repository.

tools.name: comment_on_issue_or_pr
tools.description: Add a comment to an existing issue or pull request (GitHub treats PR comments the same way as issue comments).

tools.name: close_pull_request_or_issue
tools.description: Close an open pull request or issue in a connected GitHub repository (without merging). Requires an explicit confirm=true on a second call — the first call only previews the close.

confirm.description: Set true on a second call to actually close. First call (default) only previews.
confirm.title: Confirm

tools.name: get_workflow_runs
tools.description: List recent GitHub Actions workflow runs for a connected repository — status, conclusion, and branch of each.

tools.name: trigger_workflow_dispatch
tools.description: Trigger an existing GitHub Actions workflow in a connected repository — the workflow must already declare a workflow_dispatch trigger (this doesn't create or modify workflows, only runs one that's already there). This is the 'deploy from chat' path: it runs whatever CI/CD the user already set up.

workflow.description: Workflow file name (e.g. 'deploy.yml') or numeric workflow ID — from get_workflow_runs
workflow.title: Workflow

ref.description: Branch or tag to run the workflow on
ref.title: Ref

inputs.description: Workflow input parameters, matching the workflow_dispatch inputs it declares
inputs.title: Inputs

tools.name: create_branch
tools.description: Create a new branch in a connected GitHub repository, from an existing branch/tag/commit (defaults to the repo's default branch).

from_ref.description: Branch, tag, or commit SHA to branch from. Empty = the repo's default branch.
from_ref.title: From Ref

tools.name: create_or_update_file
tools.description: Create or update a single file in a connected GitHub repository — this IS a commit (one file per call).

path.description: File path within the repo, e.g. 'src/app.py'
path.title: Path

content.description: New full file content (plain text — encoded to base64 internally)
content.title: Content

branch.description: Branch to commit to, e.g. 'main' or a feature branch
branch.title: Branch

tools.name: delete_branch
tools.description: Delete a branch in a connected GitHub repository. Irreversible if the branch has no other pointer to it, so it requires an explicit confirm=true on a second call — the first call only previews the deletion.

confirm.description: Set true on a second call to actually delete. First call (default) only previews.
confirm.title: Confirm

secrets.name: github_app_slug
secrets.description: The GitHub App's URL slug, e.g. 'webbee-imperal' for github.com/apps/webbee-imperal — shown in the App's settings URL on github.com. Public by nature (it's part of a public URL), but declared as a secret for consistency with the rest of the App's identity and to keep it configurable without a code change.

secrets.name: github_app_id
secrets.description: Numeric GitHub App ID, shown at the top of the App's settings page on github.com (Developer settings → GitHub Apps → your app).

secrets.name: github_app_private_key
secrets.description: PEM-encoded private key generated for the GitHub App (Developer settings → GitHub Apps → your app → Private keys → Generate a private key). Used to sign short-lived JWTs that are exchanged for per-installation access tokens — never sent to GitHub directly, never logged, never returned to chat.

secrets.name: github_webhook_secret
secrets.description: Shared secret configured in the GitHub App's Webhook settings, used to verify that incoming setup-callback requests really come from GitHub (HMAC-SHA256 signature check) before trusting them.
